mentions.raw_name: Ser Goodwin's friend (unnamed squire who flinched from killing)
The Faith of the Seven (the Maiden — gives Ser Galladon the Just Maid; the Mother — "Mother have mercy" spoken by Timeon; "the Mother had delivered Connington to her")
First Men (blood of the First Men in Crackclaw Point)
Oathkeeper (Valyrian steel sword with ornate scabbard, row of lion's heads)
The Just Maid (legendary enchanted sword of Ser Galladon)
Shagwell's triple morningstar (three spiked balls on chains, wooden haft)
Timeon's throwing spears (two: one long, one short and thick)
Dick Crabb's dinted halfhelm (spotted with rust)
Timeon's iron halfhelm (wrapped in stained red silk)
Two gold dragons (Brienne's payment, tossed in grave)
"The Bear and the Maiden Fair" (Nimble Dick sings a snatch: "A bear there was, a bear, a bear, all black and brown, and covered with hair")

mentions.raw_name: The Just Maid (enchanted sword — "no common sword could check her, nor any shield withstand her kiss")
Squishers (folklore — scaled creatures with green teeth, webbed feet; First Men supposedly killed them all)
Valyrian steel (Oathkeeper described as a grey blur, alive in her hands)
Aegon's Conquest (Visenya's campaign to take Crackclaw Point)
Robert's Rebellion / Battle of the Trident (Crabbs, Brunes, Boggses fought with Rhaegar)
War of the Five Kings (ongoing; Lord Brune's sons gone to war; Dick is a deserter)
War in the Stepstones (Ser Goodwin's youth)
Knight (Ser Clarence Crabb, Ser Galladon, Ser Goodwin, Ser Hyle, Ser Creighton, Ser Illifer, Ser Ronnet)
Lord (Lord Brune, Lord Lucifer Hardy, Lord Randyll Tarly, Brienne's father)
Squire (Podrick; Ser Goodwin as a young man)
Lord of Harrenhal (Vargo Hoat's claimed title)
